quality.rationale: 需沿闭合FRW宇宙过去光锥建立并求解非平凡积分，串联参数化解、零测地线方程与三角化简，对应真实宇宙学可观测体积计算场景。

origin.question: 19.23 A closed $(\mathbf{k} = 1)$ Friedmann universe has Hubble constant $H_0$ and deceleration parameter $q_0$ . Assume that the universe has always been matter dominated.(b) What is the total proper volume that we see, looking out into the sky?
origin.solution: (b) Our past light cone sweeps outward a proper radial distance $c \, dt$ in a time $dt$ (see figure). From the metric, the proper area of a 2-sphere is $4\pi r^2 R^2$ , where $r$ and $R$ are both functions of $t$ along the light cone. Thus, the visible volume is $(c = 1 \, \text{now})$$$\mathbf {V} = \int_{0}^{\mathrm {t}_{0}} \mathrm {d t} 4 \pi \mathrm {r}^{2} (\mathrm {t}) \mathbf {R}^{2} (\mathrm {t}). \tag {3}$$The differential equation for $\mathbf{r}(t)$ follows from its definition as a null cone:$$- \mathrm {d t} = \frac {\mathrm {R} (\mathrm {t})}{\left(1 - \mathrm {r}^{2}\right)^{\frac {1}{2}}} \mathrm {d r}, \tag {4}$$and $\mathbf{r} = 0$ at $t = t_0$ . To solve Equation (4) and evaluate the integral inEquation (3), we use the solution for $\mathbf{R}(t)$ parameterized by "development angle" $\eta$ (see the solution to Problem 19.18).$$R = A (1 - \cos \eta), \quad t = A (\eta - \sin \eta) \tag {5}$$where$$A = \frac {q_{0}}{\left(2 q_{0} - 1\right)^{\frac {3}{2}} H_{0}} \tag {6}$$and the current value of $\eta$ is$$\eta_{0} = \cos^{- 1} \left(\frac {1 - q_{0}}{q_{0}}\right). \tag {7}$$Equation (4) now becomes $\mathrm{d}(\sin^{-1}\mathbf{r}) = \mathrm{d}\eta$ so that$$r = \sin (\eta_{0} - \eta) \tag {8}$$and Equation (3) becomes$$\mathbf {V} = \int_{0}^{\eta_{0}} \mathbf {A} (1 - \cos \eta) \mathrm {d} \eta 4 \pi \sin^{2} (\eta_{0} - \eta) \mathbf {A}^{2} (1 - \cos \eta)^{2} .$$This straightforward integral gives$$V = 4 \pi A^{3} \left(\frac {6 1}{8 0} \sin 2 \eta_{0} + \frac {5}{4} \eta_{0} - \frac {5}{2} \sin \eta_{0} - \frac {3}{8} \eta_{0} \cos 2 \eta_{0} + \frac {1}{3 0} \sin 3 \eta_{0}\right)$$which can be reduced with Equations (6) and (7) to a function of $\mathbf{q}_0$ and $\mathbf{H}_0$ .

cached_fact_sheet.kretschmann: \frac{R^{6} r^{6} t^{2} \left(r^{2} - 1\right) \left(\cos^{2}{\left(\theta \right)} - 2\right) \sin^{2}{\left(\theta \right)} + R^{6} r^{4} t^{2} \sin^{2}{\left(\theta \right)} + R^{2} r^{4} \left(r^{2} - 1\right)^{2} \sin^{2}{\left(\theta \right)} + R^{2} r^{2} \left(1 - r^{2}\right) \sin^{2}{\left(\theta \right)} + R^{2} r^{2} \left(1 - r^{2}\right) - r^{6} t \left(R^{2} + 4 t\right)^{2} \left(r^{2} - 1\right)^{2} \left(\sin^{4}{\left(\theta \right)} + \sin^{2}{\left(\theta \right)}\right) - r^{2} t \left(R^{2} + 4 t\right)^{2} \sin^{2}{\left(\theta \right)} - r^{2} t \left(R^{2} + 4 t\right)^{2} + t \left(r^{2} - 1\right) \left(R^{2} r^{2} + 4 t \left(r^{2} - 1\right)\right)^{2} \sin^{4}{\left(\theta \right)} + t \left(r^{2} - 1\right) \left(R^{2} r^{2} + 4 t \left(r^{2} - 1\right)\right)^{2}}{8 R^{4} r^{4} t^{5} \left(r^{2} - 1\right) \sin^{2}{\left(\theta \right)}}
